headline: Same Intuit, Half the Price. What's Actually Going On?
summary: Intuit (INTU) keeps growing. In Q2 FY26 (Jan. 2026), the company reported $4.7 billion in revenue, expanding 17% year over year. Yet, the stock had already collapsed, falling from a mid-2025 peak near $786 per share to $370 levels currently.

headline: Globant Expands Autodesk Partnership to Advance Digital Twins
summary: GLOB has been named Autodesk Tandem Digital Twin Solution Provider, expanding a 15-year partnership to accelerate digital twin adoption across industries.

headline: 1 Cash-Producing Stock Worth Your Attention and 2 We Avoid
summary: Generating cash is essential for any business, but not all cash-rich companies are great investments. Some produce plenty of cash but fail to allocate it effectively, leading to missed opportunities.

headline: Is It Time To Reconsider Intuit (INTU) After This Year’s 41% Share Price Slide
summary: Investors may be wondering if Intuit at around US$369 per share is starting to look interesting on value, or if the recent sell off is a reason to stay cautious. Over the last week the stock has fallen 9.9%, with a 16.0% decline over 30 days and a 41.3% drop year to date. This leaves the 1 year return at 37.3% lower, and the 3 and 5 year returns at 14.8% and 5.8% lower respectively. These moves come as investors reassess growth software names more broadly and as Intuit continues to attract...

headline: Rapid7, Five9, and Intuit Stocks Trade Up, What You Need To Know
summary: A number of stocks jumped in the morning session after investors moved to buy the dip in SaaS names that had become significantly oversold amid a fragile market rebound driven by cautious optimism surrounding U.S.-Iran ceasefire talks.
